element_introduction_order.element: Enemies (Gol, Rocky, Skull, Medusa, Don Medusa)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Enemy avoidance and pathfinding around threats

element_introduction_order.element: Complex spatial layouts and multi-step solutions

element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Final confrontation; puzzle complexity and enemy mastery required

typical_relief_triggers: Finding safe corridors or spacing between enemies
typical_tension_triggers: Complex spatial constraints limiting solution paths
Multiple simultaneous threats in same room
Pushing blocks into wrong positions (irreversible state)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Turn-based deliberate pacing; tension builds through room complexity rather than time pressure; relief comes from successful room completion and progression unlocks

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Hidden Pro puzzle rooms available to skilled players; signaled by unlock condition rather than spatial convention

difficulty_curve.early_game: Basic puzzle mechanics introduced with simple enemy patterns and limited spatial constraints; safe learning environment
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Increased monster complexity and redraw variations; spatial layouts become more elaborate; multi-step solutions required
difficulty_curve.late_game: Greater difficulty than predecessor Adventures of Lolo (US); complex enemy routing and spatial puzzles; Pro rooms available as extreme challenge tier
notable_spikes: Mid-to-late game transition with monster variety increases
Pro puzzle rooms unlock (4 hidden extremely challenging rooms)

notable_level_patterns.description: 50 main puzzles drawn from three Eggerland sources: Eggerland: Meikyū no Fukkatsu, Eggerland: Sōzō e no Tabidashi, and Japanese Adventures of Lolo. Includes 6 entirely new unique levels plus reordered legacy content. Similar structure to predecessor but with greater difficulty variance.

Rooms feature enemy encounters (Gol, Rocky, Skull, Medusa, Don Medusa) with distinct behavioral patterns. Redrawn monster graphics vs previous title. Requires spatial avoidance routing alongside traditional block-pushing logic.

Each room centers on collecting treasure chest(s) as primary objective. Chest placement determines required pathfinding and spatial solution logic. Rooms increase in complexity through combination of chest placement and enemy threat positioning.

4 locked Pro puzzle rooms available only to players who demonstrate mastery of main 50-room sequence. Represents extreme difficulty tier beyond standard progression. Accessible to completionists seeking additional challenge.

sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor) - Adventures of Lolo 2 (NES)
